commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: God made us in Christ. So it is through Christ once again that he has formed us anew. We are his members; he our Head.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: Even when we were dead in sins, has made us alive together with Christ, (by grace you are saved;) It declares at once their life and ours. For "those who were dead in sins are quickened together with Christ". Sed non falsum dixit Dominus; revera enim opera dissolvit cupiditatis, avaritiam, contentionem, gloriae cupiditatem, mulierum insanum amorem, paedicatum, ingluviem, luxum et profusionem, et quae sunt his similia. Horum autem ortus, est animae interitus: siquidem "delictis mortui "efficimur.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Quickened us together in Christ. Faith, baptism, and grace, are pledges of our future resurrection and glorification in heaven. Our present conversion is also a kind of resurrection. The time is come, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear shall live. (St. Augustine upon St. John, No. 7.)

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: The sufferings of the present time are not worthy to be compared with the future glory that will be revealed in us. If so, we are saved by grace rather than works, for we can give God nothing in return for what he has bestowed on us. .

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Even when we were dead in sins, has made us alive together with Christ, (by grace you are saved;) Again is Christ introduced, and it is a matter well worthy of our belief, because if the Firstfruits live, so do we also. He has quickened both Him, and us. Do you see that all this is said of Christ incarnate. Beholdest thou the exceeding greatness of His power to us-ward who believe. Ephesians 1:19 Them that were dead, them that were children of wrath, them has he quickened. Beholdest thou the hope of his calling.
